root_text.sn54.13:1.2: Atha kho āyasmā ānando yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho āyasmā ānando bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn54.13:1.3: “atthi nu kho, bhante, ekadhammo bhāvito bahulīkato cattāro dhamme paripūreti, cattāro dhammā bhāvitā bahulīkatā satta dhamme paripūrenti, satta dhammā bhāvitā bahulīkatā dve dhamme paripūrentī”ti?
root_text.sn54.13:2.1: “Atthi kho, ānanda, ekadhammo bhāvito bahulīkato cattāro dhamme paripūreti, cattāro dhammā bhāvitā bahulīkatā satta dhamme paripūrenti, satta dhammā bhāvitā bahulīkatā dve dhamme paripūrentī”ti.
root_text.sn54.13:3.1: “Katamo pana, bhante, ekadhammo bhāvito bahulīkato cattāro dhamme paripūreti, cattāro dhammā bhāvitā bahulīkatā satta dhamme paripūrenti, satta dhammā bhāvitā bahulīkatā dve dhamme paripūrentī”ti?
root_text.sn54.13:3.2: “Ānāpānassatisamādhi kho, ānanda, ekadhammo bhāvito bahulīkato cattāro satipaṭṭhāne paripūreti, cattāro satipaṭṭhānā bhāvitā bahulīkatā satta bojjhaṅge paripūrenti, satta bojjhaṅgā bhāvitā bahulīkatā vijjāvimuttiṁ paripūrenti.
root_text.sn54.13:4.1: Kathaṁ bhāvito, ānanda, ānāpānassatisamādhi kathaṁ bahulīkato cattāro satipaṭṭhāne paripūreti?
root_text.sn54.13:4.2: Idhānanda, bhikkhu araññagato vā rukkhamūlagato vā suññāgāragato vā nisīdati pallaṅkaṁ ābhujitvā ujuṁ kāyaṁ paṇidhāya parimukhaṁ satiṁ upaṭṭhapetvā.
root_text.sn54.13:4.4: Dīghaṁ vā assasanto ‘dīghaṁ assasāmī’ti pajānāti, dīghaṁ vā passasanto ‘dīghaṁ passasāmī’ti pajānāti …pe…
root_text.sn54.13:5.1: Yasmiṁ samaye, ānanda, bhikkhu dīghaṁ vā assasanto ‘dīghaṁ assasāmī’ti pajānāti, dīghaṁ vā passasanto ‘dīghaṁ passasāmī’ti pajānāti;
root_text.sn54.13:5.3: ‘passambhayaṁ kāyasaṅkhāraṁ assasissāmī’ti sikkhati, ‘passambhayaṁ kāyasaṅkhāraṁ passasissāmī’ti sikkhati—
root_text.sn54.13:5.4: kāye kāyānupassī, ānanda, bhikkhu tasmiṁ samaye viharati ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ.
root_text.sn54.13:5.7: Tasmātihānanda, kāye kāyānupassī bhikkhu tasmiṁ samaye viharati ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ.
root_text.sn54.13:6.1: Yasmiṁ samaye, ānanda, bhikkhu ‘pītippaṭisaṁvedī assasissāmī’ti sikkhati,
root_text.sn54.13:6.4: ‘passambhayaṁ cittasaṅkhāraṁ assasissāmī’ti sikkhati, ‘passambhayaṁ cittasaṅkhāraṁ passasissāmī’ti sikkhati—
root_text.sn54.13:6.5: vedanāsu vedanānupassī, ānanda, bhikkhu tasmiṁ samaye viharati ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ.
root_text.sn54.13:6.7: Vedanāññatarāhaṁ, ānanda, etaṁ vadāmi, yadidaṁ—assāsapassāsānaṁ sādhukaṁ manasikāraṁ.
root_text.sn54.13:6.8: Tasmātihānanda, vedanāsu vedanānupassī bhikkhu tasmiṁ samaye viharati ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ.
root_text.sn54.13:7.1: Yasmiṁ samaye, ānanda, bhikkhu ‘cittappaṭisaṁvedī assasissāmī’ti sikkhati, ‘cittappaṭisaṁvedī passasissāmī’ti sikkhati;
root_text.sn54.13:7.4: ‘vimocayaṁ cittaṁ assasissāmī’ti sikkhati, ‘vimocayaṁ cittaṁ passasissāmī’ti sikkhati—
root_text.sn54.13:7.5: citte cittānupassī, ānanda, bhikkhu tasmiṁ samaye viharati ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ.
root_text.sn54.13:7.7: Nāhaṁ, ānanda, muṭṭhassatissa asampajānassa ānāpānassatisamādhibhāvanaṁ vadāmi.
root_text.sn54.13:7.8: Tasmātihānanda, citte cittānupassī bhikkhu tasmiṁ samaye viharati ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ.
root_text.sn54.13:8.5: dhammesu dhammānupassī, ānanda, bhikkhu tasmiṁ samaye viharati ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ.
root_text.sn54.13:8.6: So yaṁ taṁ hoti abhijjhādomanassānaṁ pahānaṁ taṁ paññāya disvā sādhukaṁ ajjhupekkhitā hoti.
root_text.sn54.13:8.7: Tasmātihānanda, dhammesu dhammānupassī bhikkhu tasmiṁ samaye viharati ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ.
root_text.sn54.13:9.1: Evaṁ bhāvito kho, ānanda, ānāpānassatisamādhi evaṁ bahulīkato cattāro satipaṭṭhāne paripūreti.
root_text.sn54.13:10.1: Kathaṁ bhāvitā cānanda, cattāro satipaṭṭhānā kathaṁ bahulīkatā satta bojjhaṅge paripūrenti?
root_text.sn54.13:10.2: Yasmiṁ samaye, ānanda, bhikkhu kāye kāyānupassī viharati—
root_text.sn54.13:10.3: upaṭṭhitāssa tasmiṁ samaye bhikkhuno sati hoti asammuṭṭhā.
root_text.sn54.13:10.4: Yasmiṁ samaye, ānanda, bhikkhuno upaṭṭhitā sati hoti asammuṭṭhā—satisambojjhaṅgo tasmiṁ samaye bhikkhuno āraddho hoti, satisambojjhaṅgaṁ tasmiṁ samaye bhikkhu bhāveti, satisambojjhaṅgo tasmiṁ samaye bhikkhuno bhāvanāpāripūriṁ gacchati.
root_text.sn54.13:11.1: So tathā sato viharanto taṁ dhammaṁ paññāya pavicinati pavicarati parivīmaṁsamāpajjati.
root_text.sn54.13:11.2: Yasmiṁ samaye, ānanda, bhikkhu tathā sato viharanto taṁ dhammaṁ paññāya pavicinati pavicarati parivīmaṁsamāpajjati—
root_text.sn54.13:11.3: dhammavicayasambojjhaṅgo tasmiṁ samaye bhikkhuno āraddho hoti, dhammavicayasambojjhaṅgaṁ tasmiṁ samaye bhikkhu bhāveti, dhammavicayasambojjhaṅgo tasmiṁ samaye bhikkhuno bhāvanāpāripūriṁ gacchati.
root_text.sn54.13:12.1: Tassa taṁ dhammaṁ paññāya pavicinato pavicarato parivīmaṁsamāpajjato āraddhaṁ hoti vīriyaṁ asallīnaṁ.
root_text.sn54.13:12.2: Yasmiṁ samaye, ānanda, bhikkhuno taṁ dhammaṁ paññāya pavicinato pavicarato parivīmaṁsamāpajjato āraddhaṁ hoti vīriyaṁ asallīnaṁ—
root_text.sn54.13:12.3: vīriyasambojjhaṅgo tasmiṁ samaye bhikkhuno āraddho hoti, vīriyasambojjhaṅgaṁ tasmiṁ samaye bhikkhu bhāveti, vīriyasambojjhaṅgo tasmiṁ samaye bhikkhuno bhāvanāpāripūriṁ gacchati.
root_text.sn54.13:13.2: Yasmiṁ samaye, ānanda, bhikkhuno āraddhavīriyassa uppajjati pīti nirāmisā—
root_text.sn54.13:13.3: pītisambojjhaṅgo tasmiṁ samaye bhikkhuno āraddho hoti, pītisambojjhaṅgaṁ tasmiṁ samaye bhikkhu bhāveti, pītisambojjhaṅgo tasmiṁ samaye bhikkhuno bhāvanāpāripūriṁ gacchati.
root_text.sn54.13:14.2: Yasmiṁ samaye, ānanda, bhikkhuno pītimanassa kāyopi passambhati, cittampi passambhati—
root_text.sn54.13:14.3: passaddhisambojjhaṅgo tasmiṁ samaye bhikkhuno āraddho hoti, passaddhisambojjhaṅgaṁ tasmiṁ samaye bhikkhu bhāveti, passaddhisambojjhaṅgo tasmiṁ samaye bhikkhuno bhāvanāpāripūriṁ gacchati.
root_text.sn54.13:15.2: Yasmiṁ samaye, ānanda, bhikkhuno passaddhakāyassa sukhino cittaṁ samādhiyati—
root_text.sn54.13:15.3: samādhisambojjhaṅgo tasmiṁ samaye bhikkhuno āraddho hoti, samādhisambojjhaṅgaṁ tasmiṁ samaye bhikkhu bhāveti, samādhisambojjhaṅgo tasmiṁ samaye bhikkhuno bhāvanāpāripūriṁ gacchati.
root_text.sn54.13:16.1: So tathāsamāhitaṁ cittaṁ sādhukaṁ ajjhupekkhitā hoti.
root_text.sn54.13:16.2: Yasmiṁ samaye, ānanda, bhikkhu tathāsamāhitaṁ cittaṁ sādhukaṁ ajjhupekkhitā hoti—
root_text.sn54.13:16.3: upekkhāsambojjhaṅgo tasmiṁ samaye bhikkhuno āraddho hoti, upekkhāsambojjhaṅgaṁ tasmiṁ samaye bhikkhu bhāveti, upekkhāsambojjhaṅgo tasmiṁ samaye bhikkhuno bhāvanāpāripūriṁ gacchati.
root_text.sn54.13:17.4: upaṭṭhitāssa tasmiṁ samaye bhikkhuno sati hoti asammuṭṭhā.
root_text.sn54.13:17.5: Yasmiṁ samaye, ānanda, bhikkhuno upaṭṭhitā sati hoti asammuṭṭhā—
root_text.sn54.13:17.6: satisambojjhaṅgo tasmiṁ samaye bhikkhuno āraddho hoti, satisambojjhaṅgaṁ tasmiṁ samaye bhikkhu bhāveti, satisambojjhaṅgo tasmiṁ samaye bhikkhuno bhāvanāpāripūriṁ gacchati.
root_text.sn54.13:18.1: So tathāsamāhitaṁ cittaṁ sādhukaṁ ajjhupekkhitā hoti.
root_text.sn54.13:18.2: Yasmiṁ samaye, ānanda, bhikkhu tathāsamāhitaṁ cittaṁ sādhukaṁ ajjhupekkhitā hoti—
root_text.sn54.13:18.3: upekkhāsambojjhaṅgo tasmiṁ samaye bhikkhuno āraddho hoti, upekkhāsambojjhaṅgaṁ tasmiṁ samaye bhikkhu bhāveti, upekkhāsambojjhaṅgo tasmiṁ samaye bhikkhuno bhāvanāpāripūriṁ gacchati.
root_text.sn54.13:18.4: Evaṁ bhāvitā kho, ānanda, cattāro satipaṭṭhānā evaṁ bahulīkatā satta bojjhaṅge paripūrenti.
root_text.sn54.13:19.1: Kathaṁ bhāvitā, ānanda, satta bojjhaṅgā kathaṁ bahulīkatā vijjāvimuttiṁ paripūrenti?
root_text.sn54.13:19.2: Idhānanda, bhikkhu satisambojjhaṅgaṁ bhāveti vivekanissitaṁ virāganissitaṁ nirodhanissitaṁ vossaggapariṇāmiṁ,
root_text.sn54.13:19.4: upekkhāsambojjhaṅgaṁ bhāveti vivekanissitaṁ virāganissitaṁ nirodhanissitaṁ vossaggapariṇāmiṁ.
root_text.sn54.13:19.5: Evaṁ bhāvitā kho, ānanda, satta bojjhaṅgā evaṁ bahulīkatā vijjāvimuttiṁ paripūrentī”ti.

translation_text.sn54.13:1.2: Then Venerable Ānanda went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and said to him:
translation_text.sn54.13:1.3: “Sir, is there one thing that, when developed and cultivated, fulfills four things; and those four things, when developed and cultivated, fulfill seven things; and those seven things, when developed and cultivated, fulfill two things?”
translation_text.sn54.13:3.2: “Immersion due to mindfulness of breathing is one thing that, when developed and cultivated, fulfills the four kinds of mindfulness meditation. And the four kinds of mindfulness meditation, when developed and cultivated, fulfill the seven awakening factors. And the seven awakening factors, when developed and cultivated, fulfill knowledge and freedom.
translation_text.sn54.13:4.1: And how is mindfulness of breathing developed and cultivated so as to fulfill the four kinds of mindfulness meditation?
translation_text.sn54.13:4.2: It’s when a mendicant—gone to a wilderness, or to the root of a tree, or to an empty hut—sits down cross-legged, sets their body straight, and establishes mindfulness in their presence.
translation_text.sn54.13:4.3: Just mindful, they breathe in. Mindful, they breathe out.
translation_text.sn54.13:4.4: Breathing in heavily they know: ‘I’m breathing in heavily.’ Breathing out heavily they know: ‘I’m breathing out heavily.’ …
translation_text.sn54.13:4.5: They practice like this: ‘I’ll breathe in observing letting go.’ They practice like this: ‘I’ll breathe out observing letting go.’
translation_text.sn54.13:5.1: There’s a time when a mendicant is breathing in heavily, and they know: ‘I’m breathing in heavily.’ Breathing out heavily they know: ‘I’m breathing out heavily.’
translation_text.sn54.13:5.3: They practice like this: ‘I’ll breathe in stilling the physical process.’ They practice like this: ‘I’ll breathe out stilling the physical process.’
translation_text.sn54.13:5.4: At such a time a mendicant is meditating by observing an aspect of the body—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.sn54.13:5.6: Because the breath is a certain aspect of the body, I say.
translation_text.sn54.13:5.7: Therefore, at such a time a mendicant is meditating by observing an aspect of the body—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.sn54.13:6.1: There’s a time when a mendicant practices like this: ‘I’ll breathe in experiencing rapture …
translation_text.sn54.13:6.4: They practice like this: ‘I’ll breathe in stilling the mental process.’ They practice like this: ‘I’ll breathe out stilling the mental process.’
translation_text.sn54.13:6.5: At such a time a mendicant is meditating by observing an aspect of feelings—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.sn54.13:6.7: Because careful application of mind to the in-breaths and out-breaths is a certain aspect of feelings, I say.
translation_text.sn54.13:6.8: Therefore, at such a time a mendicant is meditating by observing an aspect of feelings—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.sn54.13:7.1: There’s a time when a mendicant practices like this: ‘I’ll breathe in experiencing the mind.’ They practice like this: ‘I’ll breathe out experiencing the mind.’
translation_text.sn54.13:7.2: They practice like this: ‘I’ll breathe in gladdening the mind …
translation_text.sn54.13:7.4: freeing the mind.’ They practice like this: ‘I’ll breathe out freeing the mind.’
translation_text.sn54.13:7.5: At such a time a mendicant is meditating by observing an aspect of the mind—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.sn54.13:7.7: Because there is no development of immersion due to mindfulness of breathing for someone who is unmindful and lacks awareness, I say.
translation_text.sn54.13:7.8: Therefore, at such a time a mendicant is meditating by observing an aspect of the mind—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.sn54.13:8.1: There’s a time when a mendicant practices like this: ‘I’ll breathe in observing impermanence …
translation_text.sn54.13:8.4: letting go.’ They practice like this: ‘I’ll breathe out observing letting go.’
translation_text.sn54.13:8.5: At such a time a mendicant is meditating by observing an aspect of principles—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.sn54.13:8.6: Having seen with wisdom the giving up of covetousness and displeasure, they watch closely over with equanimity.
translation_text.sn54.13:8.7: Therefore, at such a time a mendicant is meditating by observing an aspect of principles—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.sn54.13:9.1: That’s how immersion due to mindfulness of breathing is developed and cultivated so as to fulfill the four kinds of mindfulness meditation.
translation_text.sn54.13:10.1: And how are the four kinds of mindfulness meditation developed and cultivated so as to fulfill the seven awakening factors?
translation_text.sn54.13:10.2: Whenever a mendicant meditates by observing an aspect of the body,
translation_text.sn54.13:10.3: their mindfulness is established and lucid.
translation_text.sn54.13:10.4: At such a time, a mendicant has activated the awakening factor of mindfulness; they develop it and perfect it.
translation_text.sn54.13:11.1: As they live mindfully in this way they investigate, explore, and inquire into that principle with wisdom.
translation_text.sn54.13:11.2: At such a time, a mendicant has activated the awakening factor of investigation of principles; they develop it and perfect it.
translation_text.sn54.13:12.1: As they investigate principles with wisdom in this way their energy is roused up and unflagging.
translation_text.sn54.13:12.2: At such a time, a mendicant has activated the awakening factor of energy; they develop it and perfect it.
translation_text.sn54.13:13.1: When you’re energetic, rapture not of the flesh arises.
translation_text.sn54.13:13.2: At such a time, a mendicant has activated the awakening factor of rapture; they develop it and perfect it.
translation_text.sn54.13:14.1: When the mind is full of rapture, the body and mind become tranquil.
translation_text.sn54.13:14.2: At such a time, a mendicant has activated the awakening factor of tranquility; they develop it and perfect it.
translation_text.sn54.13:15.1: When the body is tranquil and one feels bliss, the mind becomes immersed in samādhi.
translation_text.sn54.13:15.2: At such a time, a mendicant has activated the awakening factor of immersion; they develop it and perfect it.
translation_text.sn54.13:16.1: They closely watch over that mind immersed in samādhi.
translation_text.sn54.13:16.2: At such a time, a mendicant has activated the awakening factor of equanimity; they develop it and perfect it.
translation_text.sn54.13:17.1: Whenever a mendicant meditates by observing an aspect of feelings …
translation_text.sn54.13:17.4: their mindfulness is established and lucid.
translation_text.sn54.13:17.5: At such a time, a mendicant has activated the awakening factor of mindfulness; they develop it and perfect it. …
translation_text.sn54.13:17.7: (Tell in full as for the first kind of mindfulness meditation.)
translation_text.sn54.13:18.1: They closely watch over that mind immersed in samādhi.
translation_text.sn54.13:18.2: At such a time, a mendicant has activated the awakening factor of equanimity; they develop it and perfect it.
translation_text.sn54.13:18.4: That’s how the four kinds of mindfulness meditation are developed and cultivated so as to fulfill the seven awakening factors.
translation_text.sn54.13:19.1: And how are the seven awakening factors developed and cultivated so as to fulfill knowledge and freedom?
translation_text.sn54.13:19.2: It’s when a mendicant develops the awakening factors of mindfulness,
translation_text.sn54.13:19.3: investigation of principles, energy, rapture, tranquility, immersion,
translation_text.sn54.13:19.4: and equanimity, which rely on seclusion, fading away, and cessation, and ripen as letting go.
translation_text.sn54.13:19.5: That’s how the seven awakening factors are developed and cultivated so as to fulfill knowledge and freedom.”

variant_text.sn54.13:10.3: upaṭṭhitāssa → upaṭṭhitā tassa (bj); upaṭṭhitassa (sya-all); upaṭṭhitasati (pts1ed); upaṭṭhitassati (mr)
